content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 22, 2006


Robert Cuthbert
P.O. Box 471
Lompoc, CA 93438

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-06-089

Dear Mr. Cuthbert:

This letter is in response to your request regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTIONS

1. May you receive contributions up to the limit for both the primary and general elections?

2. May you carry over contributions received in connection with the primary election for use in the general election?

3. Where are you required to file copies of your campaign statements?

CONCLUSIONS

1. Yes. The primary and general elections are treated as separate elections for purposes of the contribution limits.

2. Yes. Unspent contributions raised for the primary election may be carried over for use in the general election.

3. You must file the original and one copy of your campaign statements (Form 460) with the Secretary of State.² You also must file two copies each with the county with the largest number of registered voters in the Assembly district you seek to

_______________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
² If you raise or spend $50,000 or more, you also must file campaign statements electronically with Secretary of State.

File No. A-06-089
Page No. 2

represent, and two copies with your county of domicile (if different than the county with
the largest number of registered voters).

FACTS

You are running for the State Assembly in the 33rd district. You have no
opponent in the primary election.

ANALYSIS

Contribution Limitations

Section 82022 defines an "election" as "any primary, general, special or recall
election held in this state. The primary and general or special elections are separate
elections for purposes of this title." (Emphasis added.) Thus, separate contribution limits
apply to primary and general elections (sections 85301 and 85302), even though the
primary and general elections are for the same office. Thus, two different contributions
(up to the applicable limits) are permitted from each contributor for each election.3

Section 85317 allows a state candidate to carry over contributions received for
one election to a subsequent election for the same elective state office. The Commission
recently amended regulation 18537.1 to clarify that the carryover provision allows
contributions to be carried over from the primary to the general election for the same
office. A copy of amended regulation 18537.1 is enclosed.

Filing Locations

Section 84215 specifies where candidates and committees must file campaign
disclosure statements (Form 460). Candidates for the State Legislature and their
controlled committees must file an original and one copy with the Secretary of State, two
copies with the county with the largest number of registered voters in the affected district,
and two copies in their county of domicile. If a controlled campaign committee's address
is in a different county than the candidate's county of domicile, two copies must be filed
in the committee's county of domicile as well. (Nielsen Advice Letter, No. A-93-321.)
No more than an original and one paper copy, or two paper copies, of each campaign
statement must be filed with any one filing officer. (Section 84215(f).) However,
because the Secretary of State is the filing officer for your original campaign statements
you are also required to file statements electronically with the Secretary of State once you
have received contributions or made expenditures totaling $50,000 or more. (Section
84605(a).) Unless one of the county offices described above is Los Angeles or San
Francisco, you have no obligation to file statements in those locations.

3 You state in your letter that you have no opponent in the primary election. This does not affect
the answer to your question, provided that your name will appear on the ballot for the primary election.
(Regulation 18537.1)

File No. A-06-089
Page No. 3


If you have additional questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By:    Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

Enclosure
